mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a persistent decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if this rule shows negative rolling Rank IC over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month trend, the market anticipates tighter policy and lower real valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign of the return response is not negative in a walk-forward backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE paired with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS does not produce negative 60-day forward returns, or if either series alone fully explains the signal with no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales in RSAFS reflect robust consumer demand and revenue growth, which should lift equity prices over the next 5 trading days when demand is expanding faster than trend.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth exceeds its trailing 6-month average by 1 standard deviation
failure_condition: Reject if strong RSAFS growth episodes do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the short-horizon response is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with steady or falling CPIAUCSL signals improving real activity without overheating, a mix that should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is non-accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO shocks without CPIAUCSL acceleration fail to outperform unconditional SPX returns, or if the joint condition yields negative 20-day returns over a sustained sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing-demand weakening and broader cyclical deceleration, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal has no directional edge after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high or rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies real-rate pressure and valuation compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-plus-inflation state does not outperform random conditioning in predicting negative 20-day returns, or if the sign flips positive during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE stays flat or falls, payroll gains confirm earnings-supportive labor demand and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if payroll expansions without rising unemployment fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses significance in rolling 2-year windows.
